pattern_recognition: Matrix Manipulation, specifically rotation. This problem signals the need for understanding 2D array transformations and in-place operations.

approaches.title: Brute Force
approaches.intuition: Create a new matrix and fill it by calculating the new positions of elements based on the rotation.
approaches.algorithm: 1. Create a new n x n matrix. 2. For each element in the original matrix, calculate its new position in the rotated matrix. 3. Copy elements to the new matrix. 4. Replace original matrix with the new matrix.

approaches.title: Optimal Approach
approaches.intuition: Perform the rotation in-place by first transposing the matrix and then reversing each row.
approaches.algorithm: 1. Transpose the matrix (swap elements across the diagonal). 2. Reverse each row of the transposed matrix.

edge_cases: Empty input (n=0), single element matrix (1x1), matrices with duplicate elements, large matrices (e.g., 1000x1000), and handling of integer overflow if applicable.
common_pitfalls: 1. Forgetting to handle the in-place requirement, leading to unnecessary space usage. 2. Incorrectly calculating the new indices during rotation. 3. Not considering the case of odd vs even dimensions, which may lead to incorrect placements.
